content:
- Required gates are green: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed after the verify-image security-floor boundary move.
- Updated the bug task file to `<status>completed</status>` and `<passes>true</passes>`, ticked the acceptance criteria, and ran `/bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh`, which refreshed Ralph summaries and selected the next task.
- Next after quitting immediately: nothing further for this bug task itself; commit the staged task-finished work (including `.ralph` bookkeeping) and push, unless the commit/push already happened in this turn.

- Finished the bug task end to end: moved verify-image security floor checks to the artifact boundary, refreshed the MOLT module floors, passed `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`, updated the task to completed/passes=true, ran `task_switch.sh`, committed, and pushed.
- Final commit: `54d89b4` on `master` (`task finished bug-verify-image-arm64-has-quay-vulnerability-findings: enforce verify image security floors at the artifact boundary`).
- Extra note: exploratory `GOTOOLCHAIN=auto go test ./...` inside `cockroachdb_molt/molt` still reaches DB-backed verify packages that require Cockroach on 127.0.0.1:26257/26258; this is outside the repo's required `make test` gate, which passed.

- Read previous Ralph progress and refreshed `.ralph/current_tasks.md` / `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`.
- Verified the refreshed active-task summary contains a single candidate: story 21 task 07, a non-bug task with `<passes>false</passes>`.
- Read the task file directly and confirmed no `<blocked_by>` tag is present, so it is unblocked and becomes the mandatory next pick under the chooser precedence.
- Checked top-level repo structure to ground the project identity in the actual codebase before selecting.
- Next after quitting immediately: write `.ralph/current_task.txt` to `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/07-task-add-a-manual-image-retag-workflow-for-version-and-optional-latest.md` and hand off execution to the implementation agent.

- Created `.ralph/current_task.txt` because it was missing.
- Wrote the selected next task path: `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/07-task-add-a-manual-image-retag-workflow-for-version-and-optional-latest.md`.
- Selection reason: after refreshing Ralph summaries, there was exactly one active unblocked failing task and no unblocked failing bug tasks, so chooser precedence requires this task next.
- Next after quitting immediately: execute story 21 task 07 with the `tdd` skill and validate `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint` all stay green.

- Read saved progress, confirmed the selected task path from `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and opened the required `improve-code-boundaries` and `tdd` skill instructions.
- Confirmed this turn must follow the task-file state machine: either create/advance a plan and quit, or execute only if the task already says `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next: read the story task file, determine whether it is planning-only or execution, and append the decision back to the progress log immediately.

- Read the task file and confirmed it has no existing `<plan>` entry or execution marker, so this turn is planning-only and must stop after writing the plan.
- Inspected `.github/workflows/publish-images.yml`, workflow-specific `.github/workflows/AGENTS.md`, and story-21 history to ground the design in the current publish topology: Quay platform refs first, GHCR manifest fan-in later, and workflow verification required online.
- Found a live boundary drift: older story plans still reference Rust workflow-contract helpers that no longer exist in the repo, while workflow-local instructions now forbid creating local workflow test suites. The plan must explicitly resolve that by using one source-controlled workflow boundary plus hosted/API verification instead of reviving stale local YAML-test architecture blindly.
- Next: finish reading the improve-code-boundaries smell guidance, then write the new task plan file, add its path to the task markdown, and quit immediately.

- Created the planning artifact for story 21 task 07 at `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/07-task-add-a-manual-image-retag-workflow-for-version-and-optional-latest_plans/2026-04-20-manual-image-retag-plan.md` and added its `<plan>` path to the task markdown.
- The plan is marked `NOW EXECUTE` because the interface is now concrete: add a separate manual `workflow_dispatch` promotion workflow, treat GHCR commit-SHA refs as the public release-promotion boundary, keep Quay as the upstream publish/security gate, and verify the flow through authenticated GitHub API dispatch instead of local workflow tests.
- Key design constraints captured in the plan: required `version` input plus boolean `set_latest`, source commit implied by the dispatched `master` head SHA, full three-image promotion, no rebuild/test reruns in the manual workflow, no swallowed registry/API errors, and immediate switch back to `TO BE VERIFIED` if hosted reality proves GHCR-only promotion or implicit source SHA is wrong.
- Next after quitting immediately: resume at Slice 0, re-check the latest hosted workflow state with `/home/joshazimullah.linux/github-api-curl`, then implement and hosted-verify the manual release-tag workflow exactly as planned.

- Read saved progress, the current task markdown, and the execution plan; confirmed this turn is `NOW EXECUTE` and must follow hosted workflow verification instead of local workflow tests.
- Read `.github/workflows/AGENTS.md`, `publish-images.yml`, `README.md`, and the GitHub auth-wrapper skill; verified the live repo currently has only one active workflow (`publish-images`) and no existing manual promotion workflow online.
- Identified the clean workflow boundary to implement next: a separate `workflow_dispatch` GHCR-only promotion workflow that uses the already-published commit-SHA tags as source, installs only `skopeo`, avoids checkout/build/test steps entirely, and keeps the three-image target list in one place.
- Next if interrupted immediately: finish adding `.github/workflows/promote-image-tags.yml`, then push it so hosted dispatch verification can begin against the real GitHub Actions surface.

- Added `.github/workflows/promote-image-tags.yml` as a separate `workflow_dispatch`-only manual release workflow. It stays GHCR-only, has required `version` and boolean `set_latest` inputs, does explicit master-ref/input validation, logs into GHCR with `GITHUB_TOKEN`, and promotes the three existing GHCR commit-SHA image refs using `skopeo copy --all` without checkout/build/test steps.
- Pushed an intermediate verification commit `5daf0fc` (`Add manual GHCR image promotion workflow`) to `master` so the hosted workflow could be indexed and tested on real GitHub Actions.
- Verified online that GitHub now lists two active workflows (`publish-images` and `promote-image-tags`) and that the new push triggered `publish-images` run `24677591189` for commit `5daf0fca70c75f5927d6df365f08586ed207da49`.
- Current blocker: the upstream hosted `publish-images` run has completed all six image publish lanes successfully but `validate-long` is still in progress, so `publish-manifest` has not yet produced the GHCR commit-SHA tags needed for positive manual-promotion dispatch testing.
- Next if interrupted immediately: wait for run `24677591189` to finish, confirm GHCR commit-SHA tags for `5daf0fca70c75f5927d6df365f08586ed207da49` exist, then dispatch `promote-image-tags` twice through `/home/joshazimullah.linux/github-api-curl` (`set_latest=false` then `true`) and inspect workflow runs plus resulting tags.

- Hosted red verification exposed a real design break: `promote-image-tags` run `24678268137` on commit `5daf0fca70c75f5927d6df365f08586ed207da49` failed in `Promote published GHCR images` with `Error parsing image name \"docker://ghcr.io/runner:5daf...\": ... name invalid`.
- The workflow log shows the live repository variables currently resolve to bare names `runner`, `setup-sql`, and `verify`, so both the new manual workflow and existing `publish-manifest` logic are deriving invalid GHCR refs instead of a valid owner/package path. This contradicts the execution design assumption that the GHCR commit-SHA source surface was already a settled boundary.
- Per the task state-machine instructions, switched the plan file back from `NOW EXECUTE` to `TO BE VERIFIED` and documented the hosted finding in the plan. Execution must stop now rather than forcing the wrong registry contract.
- Next if interrupted immediately: redesign the shared GHCR image-reference boundary first by reconciling README, `publish-images.yml`, `promote-image-tags.yml`, and live repo variable naming; only then resume execution and rerun hosted dispatch verification.

- Confirmed the active task still points to a plan ending in TO BE VERIFIED, so this turn is planning/design only unless the GHCR reference boundary can be made explicit.
- Read the task markdown and current plan; the blocker is the unresolved GHCR owner/package naming contract between README, workflows, and live repo variables after the hosted dispatch failure.
- Next: inspect README and workflow files plus live GitHub variable values, then either update the plan back to NOW EXECUTE with a concrete boundary or leave it TO BE VERIFIED and stop.

- Inspected publish-images.yml, promote-image-tags.yml, README.md, workflow-local AGENTS guidance, prior story-21 plans, and improve-code-boundaries smells relevant to config drift.
- Verified the real boundary bug: both workflows currently treat mutable repository variables as if they were GHCR-ready image coordinates, but README and prior story design expect canonical source-controlled repository names plus a separate GHCR owner namespace.
- Next: patch the task plan to make that boundary explicit, switch the plan to NOW EXECUTE, and stop immediately so the next turn can execute against a stable design.

- Updated the task-07 plan to resolve the GHCR boundary explicitly: canonical source-controlled repositories are `cockroach-migrate-runner`, `cockroach-migrate-setup-sql`, and `cockroach-migrate-verify`; Quay keeps `vars.QUAY_ORGANIZATION`; GHCR uses `${{ github.repository_owner }}`.
- Switched the plan from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE because README, prior story plans, and the hosted failure together are enough to prove the mutable per-image repo variables are the wrong boundary.
- Next if interrupted immediately: resume execution from Slice 0/1 only, fix `publish-images.yml` and `promote-image-tags.yml` together around one shared image catalog, then continue with hosted push/dispatch verification.

- Read the active task and execution plan; confirmed the plan ends in NOW EXECUTE, so this turn must implement and verify unless hosted reality forces TO BE VERIFIED again.
- Re-entering execution at Slice 0/1 to inspect the current workflow files, workflow-local guidance, and working tree around the GHCR image-reference boundary before patching anything.
- Next if interrupted immediately: compare publish/promote workflow image naming against README and current git state, then patch the shared image catalog boundary and continue with hosted verification.

- Inspected workflow-local guidance, current publish/promote workflow YAML, and README; the checked-in code still derives GHCR repositories from mutable repo variables even though README teaches source-controlled `cockroach-migrate-*` package names.
- Moving into Slice 0 by checking the live GitHub workflow inventory and repo identity before editing, so the boundary fix matches the current hosted surface rather than stale assumptions.
- Next if interrupted immediately: patch both workflows to own the canonical image catalog in source, then push and verify the hosted publish/promotion runs against the corrected GHCR refs.

- Verified Slice 0 against the live GitHub Actions API: the repo only has `publish-images` and `promote-image-tags` active, so the current task scope still matches hosted reality.
- Confirmed both checked-in workflows still leak `RUNNER_IMAGE_REPOSITORY` / `SETUP_SQL_IMAGE_REPOSITORY` / `VERIFY_IMAGE_REPOSITORY` into the GHCR path, which matches the hosted failure and makes Slice 1 the correct immediate fix.
- Next if interrupted immediately: patch the shared image catalog across publish/promote workflows, then push and wait for the hosted publish run that republishes the corrected GHCR commit-SHA tags.

- Patched the workflows to remove mutable per-image repository variables from the GHCR boundary.
- Added a source-controlled reusable workflow `.github/workflows/image-catalog.yml` that owns the canonical image catalog and now feeds both `publish-images.yml` and `promote-image-tags.yml`; updated README to describe version/latest as manual promotion of those same GHCR packages.
- Next if interrupted immediately: review the YAML carefully, then commit/push the boundary fix and inspect the hosted `publish-images` run for the new commit before dispatching manual promotion again.

- Reviewed the workflow refactor locally; both workflows now consume the reusable image catalog and the mutable GHCR repository variables are gone from the edited files.
- Preparing the hosted red/green loop now: check branch/diff sanity, then push this boundary fix so GitHub can validate the reusable workflow wiring and republish canonical GHCR commit-SHA tags.
- Next if interrupted immediately: commit the workflow boundary fix and watch the resulting `publish-images` run on GitHub for any syntax/runtime failures.

- Committed the workflow boundary fix as `4878d0c` (`Fix workflow image catalog boundary`) and pushed it to `master` so GitHub can validate the reusable workflow/catalog design for real.
- Entering hosted verification: inspect the latest push-triggered `publish-images` run for commit `4878d0c`, looking first for workflow syntax acceptance and then for corrected GHCR commit-SHA publication.
- Next if interrupted immediately: keep polling the new run, inspect failing jobs/logs if any, and iterate on workflow YAML until the corrected GHCR refs publish successfully.

- Hosted push run `24678664756` for commit `4878d0c16c5e899a9c3bda75654635cc27a1b96d` is live and GitHub already resolved the referenced reusable workflow `image-catalog.yml`, so the new workflow-call boundary is syntactically accepted.
- Polling per-job state next to see whether the catalog output shape reaches the publish lanes cleanly or if a deeper hosted failure needs another YAML iteration.
- Next if interrupted immediately: inspect run `24678664756` jobs/logs, fix any hosted failure, and continue until the canonical GHCR commit-SHA refs publish successfully.

- Local acceptance gates started while hosted validation runs: `make check` passed, `make lint` passed, and `make test` is in flight.
- Hosted run `24678664756` continues past the reusable-catalog bootstrap and is actively publishing images under the canonical package names; next poll is for completion/failure details so manual promotion can be dispatched immediately after.
- Next if interrupted immediately: collect the final `make test` result, then continue polling hosted run `24678664756` until GHCR commit-SHA publication is done and dispatch the manual promotion workflow.

- `make test` failed on `readme_operator_surface_contract`: the README addition mentions `workflow`, which violates the operator-only contract and needs to be reworded or removed.
- Hosted run `24678664756` also has failed `verify` publish-image jobs, so I am reading the precise job logs now before changing code again.
- Next if interrupted immediately: patch README to satisfy the local contract and fix the hosted verify-image publish failure based on the fetched job logs, then rerun local gates and repush.

- `make check` passed and `make lint` passed, but `make test` failed in `crates/runner/tests/readme_operator_surface_contract.rs` because the README quick-start must stay operator-only and cannot mention `workflow`.
- Hosted push run `24678664756` for commit `4878d0c16c5e899a9c3bda75654635cc27a1b96d` accepted the reusable `image-catalog.yml` shape but failed the verify publish jobs (`72169668730` amd64 and `72169668711` arm64) with Quay `401 UNAUTHORIZED` when pushing `quay.io/cockroach_migrate_tool/cockroach-migrate-verify:*`.
- That proves the current design was wrong in two ways: Quay and GHCR need separate source-controlled repository-name fields, and README is the wrong surface for manual release-process documentation.
- Switched the plan file `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/07-task-add-a-manual-image-retag-workflow-for-version-and-optional-latest_plans/2026-04-20-manual-image-retag-plan.md` back from NOW EXECUTE to TO BE VERIFIED and must quit immediately per the task state machine.
- Next if interrupted immediately: redesign the catalog with separate `quay_repository` and `ghcr_repository` fields, remove release-process wording from README, then retry the workflow fix from planning mode instead of forcing the wrong boundary.

- Re-read the saved progress, active task pointer, task markdown, and the current plan file.
- Confirmed the task is in planning mode because the plan currently ends in TO BE VERIFIED after hosted evidence disproved the one-repository-per-image catalog.
- Re-read workflow-local guidance, current workflow files, README, and improve-code-boundaries smells; the real smell is one shared image_repository field being interpreted as both Quay and GHCR identity.
- Checked local history and verified the pre-refactor Quay publish path consumed per-image repository variables, while the hosted manual-dispatch failure had already shown those values resolve to bare names like runner/setup-sql/verify.
- Next if interrupted immediately: patch the plan to make the catalog explicit as quay_repository + ghcr_repository with Quay bare repo names and GHCR canonical cockroach-migrate-* names, switch the plan back to NOW EXECUTE, then quit immediately.

- Updated the task-07 plan to resolve the remaining boundary question explicitly.
- The plan now fixes the shared image catalog shape to two repository-name fields per image: quay_repository + ghcr_repository.
- Locked in the concrete mapping for the next execution turn:
  - runner -> quay_repository=runner, ghcr_repository=cockroach-migrate-runner
  - setup-sql -> quay_repository=setup-sql, ghcr_repository=cockroach-migrate-setup-sql
  - verify -> quay_repository=verify, ghcr_repository=cockroach-migrate-verify
- Also recorded that README must not carry release-process wording because readme_operator_surface_contract already proved that boundary is wrong.
- Switched `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/07-task-add-a-manual-image-retag-workflow-for-version-and-optional-latest_plans/2026-04-20-manual-image-retag-plan.md` from TO BE VERIFIED back to NOW EXECUTE.
- Next if interrupted immediately: start execution from Slice 0/1, split `.github/workflows/image-catalog.yml` into explicit Quay/GHCR repository fields, patch publish/promote workflows to consume them, then do hosted verification.

- Read previous progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill files.
- Confirmed the active task remains story-21 task 07 and the saved plan is currently in NOW EXECUTE state.
- Next: read the task markdown and execution plan in full, then inspect the workflow/test files implicated by the GHCR vs Quay repository boundary before editing.

- Read workflow-local guidance and confirmed it forbids fake local workflow tests; also read the GitHub auth wrapper skill and will use `/home/joshazimullah.linux/github-api-curl` for hosted verification.
- Inspected the current workflow files enough to confirm they still use the muddy single `image_repository` field, so the planned two-field registry boundary fix has not been applied in the working tree.
- Next: verify the latest live workflow inventory/runs via the GitHub API, then patch the shared image catalog and README wording in one small truthful boundary refactor.

- Patched the workflow boundary: `.github/workflows/image-catalog.yml` now owns separate `quay_repository` and `ghcr_repository` fields, and both publish/promote workflows were updated to consume the split fields.
- Removed the README sentence that documented the manual promotion workflow because the operator-surface contract explicitly forbids workflow/process wording there.
- Next: confirm no stale single-field image-repository references remain, rerun the README contract that previously failed, then push for hosted workflow verification.

- The workflow boundary fix and README contract fix are committed locally as `9537a8d` (`Fix workflow registry repository split`).
- The repo is on `master`; next step is pushing that commit so GitHub can run `publish-images` and prove the split `quay_repository` / `ghcr_repository` catalog works in hosted reality.
- After the push lands: inspect the new hosted run, fix any remaining workflow/runtime failure, and only then dispatch `promote-image-tags` for version/latest verification.

- Hosted push run `24679069123` for commit `9537a8d74e32c426eccc671fcaf1956360f87d6b` is live; both `verify` publish-image jobs are already green, which confirms the split Quay/GHCR repository mapping fixed the earlier Quay auth failure for verify.
- While the slower hosted validation/manifest jobs continue, I started the explicit local completion gates required by the task: `make check` first, then `make test`.
- Next: finish the local gates, wait for hosted GHCR commit-SHA publication to complete, then dispatch `promote-image-tags` with `set_latest=false` and later `true` for real API-based verification.

- Local completion gates are green: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed after the workflow boundary refactor.
- Hosted push run `24679069123` has all six `publish-image` matrix jobs green; remaining wait is for validate lanes, Quay security gate, and publish-manifest so the canonical GHCR commit-SHA refs exist.
- Next: once hosted push validation is green, dispatch `promote-image-tags` through `/home/joshazimullah.linux/github-api-curl` with `set_latest=false`, inspect the run/logs and GHCR tags, then repeat with `set_latest=true`.

- Local gates are done and green; the only remaining blocker is the hosted `validate-long` job in push run `24679069123`, which still gates Quay security + publish-manifest.
- Verified through the GitHub job API that the long run is specifically inside `Validate ultra-long repository lane`, so it is slow rather than stuck in setup.
- Next: wait for hosted publish-manifest on commit `9537a8d74e32c426eccc671fcaf1956360f87d6b`, then dispatch and verify the manual promotion workflow for version-only and version-plus-latest paths.

- Hosted run `24679069123` has now cleared `validate-fast` and `validate-long`; the current blocker is `quay-security-gate`, which must finish before `publish-manifest` can create the GHCR commit-SHA refs for `9537a8d74e32c426eccc671fcaf1956360f87d6b`.
- Local `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed already.
- Next: wait for Quay security + publish-manifest, then perform the two authenticated `workflow_dispatch` verifications (`set_latest=false`, then `set_latest=true`) and inspect resulting GHCR tags.

- Hosted push run `24679069123` did not reach GHCR manifest publication: `quay-security-gate` failed and `publish-manifest` was skipped.
- Next: inspect the completed Quay gate logs/summary to determine whether this is a real vulnerability policy hit, a scanner timeout, or some other environmental failure before deciding the next code/task action.

- Verified locally that `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all pass after commit `9537a8d74e32c426eccc671fcaf1956360f87d6b`.
- Hosted push run `24679069123` validated the split registry naming boundary (`publish-image` matrix green, validate-fast green, validate-long green) but failed before GHCR manifest publication because `quay-security-gate` timed out waiting for Quay scans to leave `queued`; `publish-manifest` was skipped.
- That disproves the current plan assumption that manual promotion can safely use the dispatched ref's implicit `github.sha` as the source image set. I switched `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/07-task-add-a-manual-image-retag-workflow-for-version-and-optional-latest_plans/2026-04-20-manual-image-retag-plan.md` back to TO BE VERIFIED and must stop now. Next resume should redesign source selection around an actually published GHCR ref (explicit source SHA or truthful latest-published discovery).

- Read prior progress, `.ralph/current_task.txt`, the active task markdown, the required `tdd` skill, and the `improve-code-boundaries` skill plus its smell inventory.
- Confirmed the saved plan footer is currently `TO BE VERIFIED`, so this turn is still in planning mode under the Ralph task state machine.
- Verified the code already contains the split registry boundary (`quay_repository` + `ghcr_repository`) and the remaining design gap is the manual workflow source selection, which still assumes `${GITHUB_SHA}` is already published to GHCR.
- Next: inspect workflow-local guidance and the hosted/manual workflow state enough to redesign promotion around an explicitly published source ref, then update the plan and stop immediately.

- Re-read workflow-local instructions, the GitHub API auth wrapper skill, the live workflow files, and the exact plan footer/state.
- Verified via live GitHub API that `promote-image-tags` has one failed dispatch run and `publish-images` currently has zero successful runs on `master`, so `${GITHUB_SHA}` is not a truthful source selector for promotion.
- Updated `.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/07-task-add-a-manual-image-retag-workflow-for-version-and-optional-latest_plans/2026-04-20-manual-image-retag-plan.md` to keep the split registry catalog, add an explicit "latest successful publish-images run on master" source-selection boundary, require `actions: read` for that lookup, and record the resolved `source_sha` in workflow summaries.
- Switched the saved plan footer from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE` because the source-selection design is now explicit enough to implement.
- Next if interrupted immediately: start execution from Slice 0 and Slice 1 by patching `.github/workflows/promote-image-tags.yml` to resolve `source_sha` from the latest successful `publish-images` run, then push and continue hosted verification.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, and the required improve-code-boundaries and tdd skill files from disk.
- Confirmed the active task is story-21 task 07 and prior state says the plan footer is NOW EXECUTE, so this turn should resume execution rather than create a new plan.
- Next: read the task markdown and the current execution plan in full, then inspect the workflow files and tests implicated by the remaining source_sha boundary.

- Completed Slice 0 RED/GREEN evidence gathering against live GitHub Actions: workflow inventory still has image-catalog, publish-images, and promote-image-tags active; publish-images has zero successful push runs on master; the only promote-image-tags dispatch run failed.
- Patched `.github/workflows/promote-image-tags.yml` to add `actions: read`, resolve `source_sha` from the latest successful `publish-images` push run on master via the GitHub API, fail loudly when none exists, and use that `source_sha` for all GHCR promotion refs and summary output.
- Next: run local gates on the workflow edit, commit/push the fix, then continue hosted verification until publish-images succeeds and manual promotion can be dispatched for version-only and version-plus-latest cases.

- Verified hosted job breakdown for publish-images run `24679069123`: all six publish-image jobs and both validation lanes were green; the only blocker was `quay-security-gate`, which failed and skipped `publish-manifest`.
- Started inspecting the Quay gate implementation and hosted logs to determine whether the remaining blocker is a workflow design bug or just transient registry scanner latency.
- Next: use the gate logs to decide whether publish-images needs a code change before pushing the promote workflow fix, because manual promotion cannot succeed until at least one publish-images push run on master reaches publish-manifest.

- Hosted Quay gate logs showed a real workflow defect: the gate uses hard-coded magic numbers (`60` attempts, `sleep 10`) and failed after 10 minutes while most Quay scans were still merely `queued`, not failed.
- Patched `.github/workflows/publish-images.yml` so the Quay gate timeout is explicit and source-controlled (`QUAY_SCAN_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS=10`, `QUAY_SCAN_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=1800`), validates those values, reports them in the summary, and computes attempts from them instead of smearing magic numbers through the loop.
- Next: run local repo gates, update the task/plan checkboxes if the design still holds, commit/push both workflow fixes, then continue hosted validation until a publish-images run succeeds and the manual promotion workflow can be dispatched twice.

- Local completion gates on the current tree are green again after the workflow changes: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed.
- Updated the saved plan to mark Slice 0 and Slice 1 complete and recorded that hosted execution should now continue from Slice 2 with the Quay timeout budget fix and the promote workflow source_sha fix.
- Next: commit the current workflow changes plus Ralph bookkeeping, push to master, then watch the hosted publish-images run and continue into manual dispatch verification.

- Committed the workflow fixes as `b60bab7` and pushed `master` to trigger real hosted verification.
- Next: monitor the new publish-images run for commit `b60bab7` until it either reaches publish-manifest or exposes another hosted defect, then continue into manual workflow dispatch verification.

- Pushed commit `b60bab7` and confirmed the new hosted publish-images run `24680744470` started for head SHA `b60bab7b97773ff598afcd41944d7624a14f90e6`.
- All six `publish-image` jobs are now green in that run, so the split Quay/GHCR catalog boundary and publish-side image naming are fully validated in hosted reality.
- Remaining blocker is hosted timing only: `validate-fast`/`validate-long` must finish, then the Quay gate must prove the longer timeout budget is sufficient so `publish-manifest` can finally publish GHCR commit-SHA refs; after that, dispatch `promote-image-tags` twice and verify package tags through the GitHub package API.

- Hosted run `24680744470` has now cleared both validation lanes and all six publish-image jobs; the only live step left is `quay-security-gate` started at `2026-04-20T17:44:35Z`.
- The current hosted proof point is whether the new 1800-second Quay scan budget is enough to let the gate finish and unlock `publish-manifest`; no new local code change is justified until that outcome is known.
- Next: watch the live Quay gate logs, then either continue to manual dispatch verification on success or capture the exact second timeout/failure payload if it still fails.
